title: The Harborwatch Dispute
briefing: A Compact checkpoint at the harbor gate is being shaken down nightly. Three wardens hurt last week. They want it stopped quietly — nothing in the Register.

midQuestBeats.label: The Tallow Ring has moved up their timeline. They are coming tonight.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The Ring learned about the Compact's request. They are accelerating their response — you have less time than expected.

midQuestBeats.label: The watchpost is abandoned. Someone cleared it out before you arrived.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: No one is at the Harborwatch post. The dispute may run deeper than a missing guard.

midQuestBeats.label: The shakedown crew is on the gate. Three Ring men, armed.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Contact at the Harborwatch post. The Ring's men are still here — the clearing story was a lie told to the Compact to buy time.

midQuestBeats.label: Backup arrived from the dockside. Someone gave the crew warning.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Additional Ring men moved in. The post has more protection than the Compact knew — this was never just a shakedown.

aftermathText: The checkpoint holds. Three Ring men in the morgue, two more who decided tonight was not the night to argue.

title: The Missing Ledger
briefing: A Court notary lost a sealed ledger between two counting houses. It never arrived. Someone has it. Identify who is holding it now before the contents become inconvenient for the wrong people.

midQuestBeats.label: Lady Sorn has sent a message: the ledger is worth more dead than alive.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A courier delivered a warning — burn the ledger or lose your house. Lady Sorn is not waiting for the Court's decision.

midQuestBeats.label: The ledger chamber is not empty. Someone is already here.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A Compact agent is already inside the archive. He has not noticed you yet.

midQuestBeats.label: Three transfers in four days. The ledger is being used as leverage before it disappears.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The trail leads to a counting house in the lower Pale. Someone is circulating the ledger deliberately — it has been moved twice since it was first reported missing.

midQuestBeats.label: Inside the archive. The ledger is here. So is a Compact agent who has not noticed you yet.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The final address is a locked archive room beneath the Notary Quarter. The ledger is on the desk. The agent is reading from it.

aftermathText: The ledger is back where it belongs, though the notary asks no questions about how it was lost in the first place.

title: League Clearance: The Long Road
briefing: A League engineer's route was hit three times already and the schematics are still in play. The League wants the ambush crew broken before the next move. Four people minimum, professionals only.
aftermathText: The engineer reaches the foundry gates. The ambush crew is scattered; the League will count this as a victory.

midQuestBeats.label: The contact point is empty. Someone cleared it before you arrived.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: No one is waiting at the rendezvous. The target may have been moved or the intel was stale.

midQuestBeats.label: Contact made. The opposition is armed and prepared.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The target is here, and they brought backup. This is going to be messy.

title: A Debt Already Paid
briefing: Someone is using a dead man's name to collect on old Ring debts. The payments are real — the claims are not. You have one lead. Work it before the next collection window closes.

midQuestBeats.label: The creditor sends word: pay in full or bring blood money.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A runner delivers a final demand from the Ring. Negotiation is no longer an option.

midQuestBeats.label: The fraud runs through a dead man's seal. Harlen Voss died eight months ago.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The debt claims use Voss's authorization mark — forged, or held in trust by someone with access to his estate. The collection is being run by someone who knows exactly what they are doing.

midQuestBeats.label: The safehouse is in the lower Hollows. One collector. Two witnesses. They have not realized who is watching.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The collection point smells like old paper and new coin. The collector has not recognized the house yet.

aftermathText: The ledger surfaces in a counting house off Northbank. Someone is using old debts as leverage.

title: The Restored Ask a Favor
briefing: They need a document retrieved from a Court archive before it is sealed. No violence — they are specific about this. Get in, find the record, leave. The Restored will handle the rest.
aftermathText: The seal is verified. The restored house now has standing to press claims against its former stewards.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Retrieve the Runaway
briefing: A bonded servant's protectors are holed up in the Warrens and refusing to yield them. The employer wants the resistance broken. No questions asked, no written record. You will not be given a name.

midQuestBeats.label: Found them. They are not alone — someone is standing watch.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The runaway took shelter with a sympathizer in the Warrens. The guard situation is not what the employer described.

midQuestBeats.label: The shelter occupant ran for help. Someone else now knows the house is here.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Noise carries in the Warrens. The sympathizer's call is going to bring attention to the safehouse.

aftermathText: The target walks out of the Warrens under a new name. The Foundry will pay for discretion.

title: A Word with Brennik
briefing: A creditor named Brennik owes the Ring and has decided not to pay. This is a short job. Make the conversation memorable without breaking anything that matters. The Ring prefers outcomes over incidents.
aftermathText: The ledger is recovered from a Ring safehouse. The debt collectors know who owes what now.

midQuestBeats.label: The contact point is empty. Someone cleared it before you arrived.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: No one is waiting at the rendezvous. The target may have been moved or the intel was stale.

midQuestBeats.label: Contact made. The opposition is armed and prepared.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The target is here, and they brought backup. This is going to be messy.

title: Nightbloom Required
briefing: Three units of nightbloom extract. No paperwork, no licensed supplier, no questions. The buyer is not available for discussion. You will need the right contacts — the Hollows is where you start.
aftermathText: The extract is delivered. The buyer asks no questions about where the nightblooms were sourced.

midQuestBeats.label: The drop point has been moved.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The contact sent a new location at the last minute. Something feels off.

midQuestBeats.label: The exchange is interrupted.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Someone else showed up for the handoff. This was a setup.

title: Safe Passage Through the Pale
briefing: A merchant's wagon needs to cross the Pale undamaged. Three prior attempts have not gone well. The cargo is not your concern. Protect the convoy from ambush and ensure it reaches its destination intact.
aftermathText: The wagon reaches the destination. The watch crew holds through the job; the Tallow Ring learns not to test this route.

midQuestBeats.label: The position is more exposed than expected.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The defensive line is thinner than briefed. More eyes are watching this spot.

midQuestBeats.label: The enemy tests the position.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A probe attack confirms they know where we are. The real assault is coming.

title: Industrial Consulting
briefing: A Foundry League forge is producing at a rate that concerns a competitor. You have been retained to address the throughput problem. The employer calls this consulting. You may call it whatever you like.

midQuestBeats.label: The forge floor is live. Workers still at their stations — this is messier than promised.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The forge runs a double crew tonight. Getting to the throughput machinery means going through people who have no part in the dispute.

midQuestBeats.label: The League flagged the intrusion. A foreman is bringing security from the east corridor.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: League security is moving in. The window for clean interference has closed.

aftermathText: The sabotage is complete. The League will trace this back to the Ring; the war escalates.

title: The House on Soot Lane
briefing: There is a ledger in an abandoned house on Soot Lane. The last team sent to retrieve it did not come back. The ledger is still wanted. The terms are the same as they were offered to the last team.
aftermathText: The ledger is found. Someone in the Hollows has been keeping accounts that should have stayed buried.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Two Houses, One Table
briefing: Two minor bond-holder houses are disputing a shared contract and the costs are mounting. A neutral third party is needed — someone with no position in the outcome. Tact is the minimum requirement. Discretion is assumed.
aftermathText: The dispute is settled. The slaver house learns that the Court does not tolerate unauthorized trafficking.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Three Days on Assessor Vorn
briefing: Follow a Compact assessor named Vorn for three days. Document his route, his contacts, and anything he takes that does not appear in the Register. Do not make contact. Do not be seen.

midQuestBeats.label: Vorn visits the archive quarter three times in two days. He is retrieving, not auditing.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Assessor Vorn is pulling sealed case files — not writing new ones. Whatever he is looking for has already been recorded somewhere.

midQuestBeats.label: Third meeting: a closed courtyard behind the eastern precinct. A document exchange. Neither party wore a mark.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Vorn handed off a sealed folder to an unmarked contact. The folder was not Compact issue. The exchange was practiced — this is not the first time.

aftermathText: Three days of surveillance yield a pattern. Vorn is pulling files, not writing them.

title: Presence Required at the Fold
briefing: A Gilded Court auction house requires visible security for an evening sale. Hold the door through the event and look like you will not be reasoned with. That is the whole job.
aftermathText: The auction ends without incident. The Court counts this as a success; the bidder who tried something does not return.

midQuestBeats.label: The position is more exposed than expected.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The defensive line is thinner than briefed. More eyes are watching this spot.

midQuestBeats.label: The enemy tests the position.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A probe attack confirms they know where we are. The real assault is coming.

title: The Warehouse on Rendmill Street
briefing: A business arrangement in an Ironworks warehouse ended badly. The employer has a warehouse contact waiting for a sealed cleanup packet before the morning inspection. The details will be obvious when you arrive.
aftermathText: The cleanup is complete. The ironworks runs smoother now; the foundry knows who to call next time.

midQuestBeats.label: The drop point has been moved.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The contact sent a new location at the last minute. Something feels off.

midQuestBeats.label: The exchange is interrupted.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Someone else showed up for the handoff. This was a setup.

title: A Name in the Magpie's Ledger
briefing: Tessaly Ash asks a favor before she speaks of Mira. A Court courier is carrying something of hers — proof, she says, that she can be trusted. Recover it without drawing attention. The price for what she knows is silence, not coin.

midQuestBeats.label: The courier's route passes through three Compact checkpoints. He is not using the registered path.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Tessaly's courier is avoiding the main roads. Whatever he carries is meant to stay off the Register.

midQuestBeats.label: The courier is in a side alley off the Pale's eastern market. He has noticed you.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The courier reached for a signal before you had to show yourself. Tessaly's price for information is trust — and she is testing whether you can earn it.

aftermathText: The proof is exactly what Tessaly said it would be — nothing dramatic, just a record that survives being checked. She reads it once, folds it away, and looks at you the way someone looks at a door they weren't sure would open. "All right," she says. "Now we talk about Mira."

title: No Names, No Grudges
briefing: The men watching the tannery don't move like Court muscle. They move like a paid crew that trains together. Find one, and find out who signs their contract.
flavorNote: Tessaly recognized the formation before you did. She has seen the Gilded Hand work before.

midQuestBeats.label: One of the watchers breaks from the pattern to make a drop. Deniable, clean, professional.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Whoever they are, they don't improvise. Every movement has a reason, and none of the reasons are personal.

midQuestBeats.label: Cornered, the operative gives up nothing about Mira — only the name of who's paying.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: "Gilded Hand. Contract work. Ask Fell if you want more than that, and he won't give it to you either." It isn't defiance. It's simply the truth: there is nothing more to give.

aftermathText: The Gilded Hand doesn't hold grudges and doesn't need to be told twice why a job matters. That is what makes them worse than the Court's own people: nobody guarding Mira has any reason to let her go, and nobody guarding her has any reason to hurt her either. It's a contract. It ends when someone pays it off or ends it by force. You now know which one this will be.

title: The Tannery Watch
briefing: Tessaly has given you the tannery's guard pattern. Chart it for three watches without being seen. Mira is moved between two rooms on a schedule — and that schedule is signed. You need to know who signed it before you make your move.

midQuestBeats.label: The watch rotates early on the second day. Someone tightened security after the courier incident.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The guard pattern changed — the courier must have been more important than Tessaly said. Or the Court knows something is coming.

midQuestBeats.label: The schedule is signed by Dalen Morke. This is not a holding — it is a transfer.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Mira is being moved in two days. Dalen Morke's name is on the order, which means someone inside the Court's transport chain is managing this personally.

midQuestBeats.label: The rotation itself is the giveaway. This isn't a Court garrison pattern — it's a contract crew's discipline.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: No Court guard stands a post like that. Whoever is actually holding this building is being paid to do it well, not ordered to do it at all.

aftermathText: Three watches, and the pattern holds: professional, unhurried, and never once careless. Whoever is running this tannery isn't afraid of you yet. That will have to change.

title: The Pale Cage
briefing: Mira is alive — held somewhere in The Pale under Gilded Court supervision. Getting her out means going through their people.
flavorNote: Tessaly says she left a note. She did not say what it contains.
openingText: Tessaly sets down the cup before she speaks. 'She is alive. The Gilded Court has her in the old tannery on the Pale's eastern edge. They are not using her yet — but they will.' She does not elaborate on what *yet* means. She does not need to.
aftermathText: Mira walks out on her own feet, which tells you something. She does not speak on the way back. When she does, it is only to say: 'I knew you would come. I did not know if that was a good thing.' She has a name for you now, too — one you have not heard before. House Valdris, she says, as if she is testing the weight of it.

midQuestBeats.label: The Court has doubled the guard rotation. They know you are coming.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A runner from the tannery's perimeter reported back: the Gilded Court has increased the guard count. Someone tipped them off.

midQuestBeats.label: Inside the tannery. Tomas Rell holds the yard while Catrin Hale keeps the holding floor.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Tomas Rell has the outer route. Catrin Hale has the holding floor. Mira is somewhere past them in the inner ring, which means going through.

midQuestBeats.label: One guard called for relief. The extraction window is closing.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A runner slipped out before the squad could cut the route. They know someone is inside. Time is now the primary enemy.

title: Days Since Open Sky
briefing: Mira counts the days aloud, still, like a habit she can't put down. Marion thinks the house is handling her return the wrong way — too many questions, not enough patience.
flavorNote: Marion has watched people come back from worse than the tannery. She has never watched it go well when the house pretended nothing happened.
openingText: "She flinches at doors closing. She's stopped answering anything directly, even things she used to just say plainly." Marion isn't asking you to fix it. "I want to know what she actually needs, instead of what's easiest for the rest of us to give her."
aftermathText: You don't get a clean answer — nobody does, from someone who spent eighteen months learning that direct answers cost something. But you learn what she needs isn't reassurance. It's a task, however small, that is entirely hers to finish or fail on her own terms. She asks for exactly one thing: a door she can close from the inside.

midQuestBeats.label: She recites the count without being asked — forty-one days, then a correction, then silence.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The number changes each time she says it, as if she is still deciding which day the counting should have started.

midQuestBeats.label: She answers a direct question directly, once, and immediately looks like she regrets it.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: It cost her something to say it plainly. Whatever this house does next should not make her pay that price twice.

title: What She Needs Now
briefing: Mira wants work. Not comfort, not watching, not being managed — a real task the house actually needs done, with her name on the outcome.
flavorNote: Curiosity was always her sharpest trait. Eighteen months of captivity didn't remove it. It just gave it somewhere darker to point.
openingText: She finds you instead of waiting to be found — a small thing, but the first time she's chosen to. "I want something to do that isn't being watched recover." She doesn't ask permission for what comes next. She states it. "Give me something real, and I'll tell you if I can still do it."
aftermathText: She finishes the task cleanly, without announcing it, and only mentions it afterward because you asked. It isn't a return to who she was before the tannery — that person may not come back at all. It's something else: proof, to herself first and the house second, that whatever she is now is still capable of finishing what she starts.

midQuestBeats.label: She's already found the flaw in the plan before you finished explaining it.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Whatever the Court did to her, they didn't touch the part of her mind that finds the gap in an argument before anyone else notices it's there.

midQuestBeats.label: For the length of the task, she doesn't count anything. Not days, not exits, not risks out loud.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: It's the first time since she came back that she's been entirely present instead of half-braced for something.

title: The Court's Answer
briefing: The Court does not forget loud raids. A seizure writ has been filed against the house — Dalen Morke, the assessor who signed Mira's transfer order, is now demanding the house's assets as restitution. Hold the door for three watches. The writ fails, but the Court now needs the debt to succeed where the raid did not.
aftermathText: The writ fails but is filed — the Court now needs the debt to succeed where the raid did not. Mira stands in the doorway behind you, silent. She has not spoken since she walked out of the tannery. When she finally does, it is only to say that the Court will not forget this either.

midQuestBeats.label: The position is more exposed than expected.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The defensive line is thinner than briefed. More eyes are watching this spot.

midQuestBeats.label: The enemy tests the position.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: A probe attack confirms they know where we are. The real assault is coming.

title: The Forger's Hand
briefing: Dael Morw left the Compact Register the day they told him to falsify a birth record, but he kept his contacts. He knows how a document like the note that broke House Valdris gets made to survive scrutiny — and who in the Register still does that kind of work.
flavorNote: Dael isn't proud of what he knows. He is certain it's the only useful thing he has left.
openingText: "A note that survives a magistrate's first look isn't drawn by an amateur. There's a house style to good forgery, same as any other trade." Dael says it like a man describing a wound that never quite closed. "I can tell you whose style this is. I can't promise you'll like knowing."
aftermathText: The hand belongs to a Register clerk who has done this kind of work for the Court twice before, always through an intermediary, never with a signature that survives. It isn't proof that reaches Cassia Vorne directly — she is too careful for that — but it is proof the note was manufactured to order, not simply mistaken or exaggerated. Dael hands over the last copy he has and doesn't ask what you'll do with it. "I kept it long enough," he says. "It's yours to carry now."

midQuestBeats.label: The clerk's technique is distinctive: aged ink over fresh paper, a filing date backdated by exactly the right margin to avoid audit.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: This is careful, practiced work. Whoever did this has done it enough times to know precisely how much backdating a Register audit will tolerate.

midQuestBeats.label: The clerk works alone but reports through a fixer who never gives a name.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The chain stops at the fixer, same as it always does with work like this. That's the whole design — deniability built in from the start.

title: The Weight of Paper
briefing: The forged note, the staged robbery, the manufactured claim — you have enough now to say the name out loud. Cassia Vorne built the case that ended House Valdris. She will meet with you. She always meets. It costs her nothing, because everything she did was, on paper, entirely correct.
flavorNote: There is no writ for what she did. Every document is in order. That is the entire point of her.

midQuestBeats.label: Every document you bring, she has already read. She filed half of them herself.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: She isn't surprised by anything you show her. She built the case; she remembers every page of it better than you do.

midQuestBeats.label: She listens to the accusation the way she listens to a filing she already expects to win.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Nothing you say changes her expression. Whatever this costs her, it isn't fear.

aftermathText: She doesn't deny any of it. Why would she — nothing you're holding would survive a magistrate's review, and she knows it before you do. "You did the work properly," she says, and it sounds like professional respect, because it is. "I'll allow that the note was ambitious. I'll allow that the timing was unkind." She stands. "I won't allow that it was wrong. House Valdris was becoming a problem I was paid to solve, and I solved it." What you leave with isn't a confession or a conviction. It's her attention — she will not underestimate this house again, and she will do this again to the next one that grows inconveniently large. That is not a victory. It's the only kind of answer someone like her was ever going to give.

title: What Was Taken
briefing: The removal chit and the margin note agree: the ledgers were not lost in the seizure. They were chosen. Someone knew exactly which pages would matter before the debt claim was even filed. Marion wants to know what you intend to do with that.
openingText: Marion turns the household ledger around so you can see the gap yourself. "You need a job done that isn't on any list I keep." She taps the removal chit against the table, once. "Someone chose which of our books to take, the night the house fell. That is not what a random seizure looks like. That is what a targeted one looks like."
aftermathText: Marion doesn't wait for you to ask the next question. "Orren Wex kept the originals. If the tally was bent before it ever reached your name, he's the only one left who'd know how." She says his name the way she says everything that costs her something — flatly, and only once.

midQuestBeats.label: The removal chit and the arrangement note describe the same night from two different rooms.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Bureau and study both point the same direction: whoever stripped the house that night had a list, not an appetite.

midQuestBeats.label: Marion has already decided what she thinks you should do. She is waiting to see if you agree.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: She hasn't said Orren's name yet. She is deciding whether you're the kind of person she can say it to.

title: Old Ledgers
briefing: Orren Wex, your former steward, is in Compact custody in The Hollows. His knowledge of House Valdris's debts could change everything — if you can get him out.
flavorNote: Marion knows where he is. She has known for weeks.
openingText: Marion does not sit when she brings this to you. She stands near the door, as if she is not certain you will want her in the room when she is done. 'Orren Wex is in Compact custody. He has been for six weeks. They are not charging him — they are keeping him available. He knows every number in the house's original accounts. If they let him talk to the right people, the debt becomes very clearly yours.'
aftermathText: Orren is older than you remembered. He holds the ledger you brought him — the copy, not the original — and spends a long time looking at the first page. 'The original debt was manipulated,' he says. 'I suspected. I could not prove it.' He can prove it now, or something close to it. What you do with that proof is a separate question — one he is too tired to answer tonight.

midQuestBeats.label: Orren is held in a converted warehouse two streets from the Compact district office. Not a formal cell.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The detention is unofficial — a holding room dressed as a debtor's residence. The Compact is keeping it quiet because the custody is not sanctioned by any Register entry.

midQuestBeats.label: Forty seconds when the door goes unobserved. The guard rotation is irregular.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Inside. Orren recognized the house's people before they had to explain. He is older than his records suggested — and more careful.

title: Three Days After
briefing: Cael died in the Harbor Ward three days after your father — officially a street robbery. Nothing was taken but his signet, and no one was ever charged. Marion never believed the report. Neither do you, not anymore.
flavorNote: Orren's proof reopened one door. Marion has been waiting to ask about this one since before you knew there was a debt to prove.
openingText: "A street robbery took his signet and nothing else, three days after your father died, in a ward with a Compact patrol every quarter hour." Marion says it the way she says numbers that don't balance. "Nobody investigated it as anything but bad luck. I want to know who decided that was the story."
aftermathText: The report was filed by a clerk who no longer works the ward, closed by a magistrate who never saw the body. What's left points to a name Cael must have found before whoever silenced him could stop him from writing it down: the same forged promissory note that broke the house. He didn't die for nothing. He died for looking at exactly the right piece of paper — and whoever gave the order was careful enough that it still can't be proven past a hired hand who is already gone.

midQuestBeats.label: The original report is thin, procedural, and closed in under a day. Too fast for a Harbor Ward robbery.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Robbery cases in the Harbor Ward take weeks to close, when they close at all. This one was signed off before the body was cold.

midQuestBeats.label: Whoever did it was a professional, not a mugger. The wound pattern says so; the missing signet was staged.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: This wasn't a robbery gone wrong. It was made to look like one — competently, and by someone with no personal stake in it.

title: The Harborwatch Reckoning
briefing: The Harborwatch dispute is unresolved — the post was cleared before you arrived. The Compact wants the name behind the order. Work the Ring lead they gave you and bring back something they can use.
flavorNote: The Compact is embarrassed. They want evidence, not a body count.
aftermathText: The Ring learns that the harbor is not theirs to shake down. The Compact notes who did the work.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: The Ledger's Second Page
briefing: You recovered the ledger, but a second page was torn free before you arrived. The Court believes a Foundry intermediary is holding it for a handoff. Get to the contact before they use it.
flavorNote: The Foundry League will not negotiate. They will however be distracted.
aftermathText: The debt is called in. The Court now has leverage over a house that thought it was secure.

midQuestBeats.label: The drop point has been moved.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The contact sent a new location at the last minute. Something feels off.

midQuestBeats.label: The exchange is interrupted.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Someone else showed up for the handoff. This was a setup.

title: Ashes Where the Ledger Was
briefing: The ledger is gone — burned before you could reach it. The Court's reaction is controlled fury. They want a likely hand behind it. Work the surviving lead and give them a name they can use.
flavorNote: A failure can still be profitable if someone else is blamed.
aftermathText: The ledger is ash. Someone wins, someone loses, and the notary loses a job either way.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Broken Muster
briefing: A League contact has word that the Iron Covenant are moving labor and steel through a private muster point in the Ironworks. They have one lead on the line. Confirm it before the next rotation and the house can move on the report.
flavorNote: The Covenant do not announce a line until the line is already theirs.
aftermathText: The covenant learns that the Foundry does not tolerate encroachment. Their expansion stalls.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Whispers in White Ink
briefing: A clerk attached to the Compact believes the Pale Sisters have begun cultivating informants around the registry rooms and chapel stores. Names are moving quietly. Work the lead before the Sisters turn rumor into leverage.
flavorNote: The Sisters do not need to own a room if every pen in it already answers to them.
aftermathText: The sisters learn that the Compact watches them closely. Their recruitment slows.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Contract Men at the Fold
briefing: The Gilded Hand have started taking private security work near the Fold and the upper counting houses. A Restored contact wants a usable read on who brought them in and where they will matter next. Work the lead quickly and the house can move early.
flavorNote: Professional rivals are still rivals. The paperwork just arrives on better paper.
aftermathText: The hand learns that the Court does not tolerate theft. Their grip loosens.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: Forn's Quiet Hiring
briefing: The Ashen Compact have begun sounding out debtors, labor captains, and the newly displaced in the Warrens. Someone wants a usable read on what Director Forn is building toward. Work the lead before those offers harden into sworn hands.
flavorNote: The Compact are most dangerous when they still look like an employer instead of a rival house.
aftermathText: The compact learns that the Ring has teeth. Their pressure campaign stalls.

midQuestBeats.label: The location is not what the briefing suggested.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The intel was incomplete. Someone has been active here recently.

midQuestBeats.label: A lead surfaces that was not in the original brief.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Something unexpected turned up. The trail runs deeper than anticipated.

title: The Vorn Files
briefing: The surveillance on Assessor Vorn yielded something. Someone wants the files back - or destroyed. The Compact is offering a substantial reward for the evidence that was never supposed to exist.
flavorNote: The files Vorn was retrieving implicate someone in the Court. That someone is willing to pay well to keep it quiet.
aftermathText: The files change hands. The buyer never reveals what was in them, but the payment speaks to the value. Vorn continues his work; someone else sleeps easier tonight.

midQuestBeats.label: The drop box is empty. Someone beat you to it.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The evidence is gone. Either the buyer got nervous or the Compact decided to clean up their own mess.

midQuestBeats.label: The buyer arrives with armed escort. They do not trust you.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The contact brings two guards. They inspect the files carefully before handing over the payment. Whatever is in those pages is worth the protection.

title: Green Corridor Supply Run
briefing: The Green Corridor is open enough for a quick run. Take a light squad, move through before the weather turns or bandits regroup. Bring back what you can carry.
aftermathText: The squad returned with supplies. The Corridor held long enough for the run.

midQuestBeats.label: Bandit scouts spotted on the ridge. They are watching the road.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Enemy movement along the corridor edge. The run is no longer clean.

midQuestBeats.label: The road is narrow and exposed. One wrong move and you are pinned.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The terrain favors ambush. Move carefully or pay the price.

midQuestBeats.label: Contact with a scavenger party. They want your supplies.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The scavengers are well armed. They have been waiting for a convoy.

title: Ashfields Border Run
briefing: The League needs supplies moved through the Ashfields border. Dangerous territory, but the pay reflects the risk. A heavy squad is recommended.
aftermathText: The Ashfields run is done. The League counts this as a favor owed.

midQuestBeats.label: A burned farmhouse yields a clue: someone is running a hidden supply line.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The Ashfields hide more than monsters. A covert operation moves through here.

midQuestBeats.label: The border checkpoint is abandoned. Someone cleared it recently.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: The checkpoint shows signs of a quick departure. Weapons left behind, no bodies.

midQuestBeats.label: A rival convoy spotted. They are armed and looking for trouble.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Another house is running the same route. They do not share the road.

title: The Unspoken Mercy
briefing: Years ago Cessa Rill altered a birth entry to save a child from a law that would have destroyed her by paperwork alone. She has never learned what became of that child, and has never let herself ask. She's asking now.
openingText: Cessa doesn't look up from her ledger of names. "Her name isn't in any record I can show you -- that was the point. But there are other ways to trace a person who was never supposed to be traceable. If you find her, I don't need to meet her. I need to know she's alive, and whether she'd call what I did mercy."
aftermathText: She is alive. Married into a tannery family, two children of her own, no idea her birth record was ever anything but ordinary. When you tell Cessa, she is quiet for a long moment. "Then it was mercy," she says, testing the word like she doesn't trust it to hold her weight. "I can live with not being thanked for it. I couldn't have lived with the other answer."

midQuestBeats.label: The altered entry itself is a dead end by design. Cessa made sure of that.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Whatever else Cessa Rill is, she is thorough. The record trail she left behind was built to end exactly where it ends.

midQuestBeats.label: A tannery family with an unremarkable daughter, unremarkably married, unremarkably alive.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: No one in this household has ever wondered about their mother's birth record. That was always the entire point of what Cessa did.

title: The Insurance Policy
briefing: Dael Morw kept copies of Register corruption he was ordered to help commit, and has been waiting years for someone he trusts enough to use them. He's decided that's you.
openingText: Dael lays out three folios, each a duplicate of the others by habit rather than need. "The assessor who gave the order is still working. Still assessing. If these get to the right desk in the Compact, that ends." He hesitates. "It also ends me, professionally, the moment anyone traces the copies back. I need to know you'll use them carefully."
aftermathText: The assessor is quietly reassigned within the week -- no trial, no public accounting, just a name removed from a roster and a case closed without ever being opened. Dael watches it happen from a safe distance and doesn't celebrate. "It's not justice," he says. "But it's the shape of consequence I was able to get. I've made my peace with that being enough, some days more than others."

midQuestBeats.label: The documents name the assessor plainly, but naming him is not the same as removing him.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Dael's copies are damning on their own terms. What they lack is a route to someone who will act on them without also burying Dael.

midQuestBeats.label: A quiet contact inside the Register agrees to move the documents, on the condition Dael's name never appears.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Not everyone in the Compact is what the Compact made them. Some of them are still keeping their own kind of ledger.

title: The Borrowed Crest
briefing: Someone paid River Ward jewelers to forge a crest ring convincing enough to make Mira trust a stranger. Old Maret has traced the metalwork. Now someone needs to trace the order.
openingText: The River Ward jeweler who cast the ring is easy enough to find and terrified enough to talk without much persuading. "A woman commissioned it. Paid in Court coin, spoke like Compact." He won't give a name. He gives a description, and the description matches someone the Gilded Hand has used before.
aftermathText: The commission traces to a Gilded Hand intermediary working under Commander Fell's authority -- confirmation that Mira's abduction was planned around her specifically, down to which stranger's face she'd be willing to trust. It is not a comfortable thing to know about your own sister. It is, at least, no longer a guess.

midQuestBeats.label: The jeweler's memory is better than his nerve. He remembers the coin purse before he remembers the face.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Court coin spent by someone who talks like the Compact. Whoever placed the order wanted it to look like ordinary noble business.

midQuestBeats.label: The description matches a Gilded Hand courier already flagged from the tannery watch.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: This was never a random ring. It was a tool, sized and shaped for exactly one target.

title: Three Evenings on the Lane
briefing: Sanna Veld recognises the garret sight lines in the watch sketch -- and remembers a man standing on the lane for three evenings before the raid. She thought he was waiting for someone else.
openingText: Sanna's description is precise in the way only guilt makes memory precise: build, coat, the way he stood like he was counting something. "Three evenings. Then the fourth, it happened." She won't say it was her fault for not reporting him. She doesn't have to. It's in every word she chooses instead.
aftermathText: The lane-watcher was a Gilded Hand scout, confirmed by a Pale Market stallholder who'd sold him the same meal three evenings running. The raid wasn't opportunistic and it wasn't sudden -- it was rehearsed, timed, and walked through in advance by someone who wanted no surprises on the night. Sanna hears the confirmation and says nothing for a long time. "I could have said something," she finally says. "I didn't know that yet, but I could have."

midQuestBeats.label: A Pale Market stallholder remembers feeding the same stranger three evenings running.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Small vendors remember faces better than anyone gives them credit for. This one remembers exactly which evenings, and exactly how the man paid.

midQuestBeats.label: The timeline lines up precisely with the raid -- three nights of watching, then the fourth night, action.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: This was not a raid of opportunity. Someone walked the lane, learned its rhythm, and waited for the night that suited them best.

title: An Offer From the Warrens
briefing: Mael Forn, Director of the Ashen Compact and once, briefly, a Valdris man himself, has sent an offer through Marion: resources and protection in exchange for a foothold on the house's diminished territory. Nothing about it is illegal. That isn't the same as it being free.
openingText: Forn's letter is precise and unsentimental: warehouse access, a standing line of credit, and quiet protection from Compact debt collectors, in exchange for allowing Ashen Compact couriers to route through Valdris-held ground without challenge. He doesn't threaten. He doesn't need to. He simply notes that the offer expires the day someone else takes the territory instead.
aftermathText: The arrangement goes through, and it helps — the credit line is real, the protection holds, and for the first time in months the house isn't the only thing standing between its people and the Compact. It also means Ashen Compact couriers now move through streets that used to answer only to House Valdris, and Forn's name is quietly attached to the house's recovery in a way that will need explaining later, to people who will not be generous about it. Marion doesn't say she told you so. She doesn't have to.

midQuestBeats.label: The terms are generous enough to be suspicious, and specific enough to have been drafted by someone who knows exactly what the house needs right now.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Forn didn't guess what the house was short on. Someone told him, or he's been watching closely enough to know without being told.

midQuestBeats.label: Marion has already run the numbers and doesn't like what accepting says about the house's position.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Marion's objection isn't moral. It's professional: taking this deal means admitting, on the record, that the house needs it.

title: What Marion Knows
briefing: Six years administering House Valdris's contracts left Marion with more knowledge of the Gilded Court's methods and finances than they know she has. She's decided you've finally earned it.
openingText: Marion lays out six years of contract memory in an evening: which Court officers can be bought, which can't, which magistrates owe favors they haven't collected yet, and which financial instruments the Court uses to launder a manufactured claim into something that survives an audit. "I kept this because knowledge with no plan behind it is just gossip with better sourcing," she says. "You have a plan now. That's the only reason you're hearing this."
aftermathText: It isn't a weapon by itself, but it's a map of where the weapons are kept. Marion watches you absorb it with the particular satisfaction of someone whose patience has finally paid a professional dividend. "I wasn't protecting a secret," she says. "I was protecting the timing of it. Those aren't the same thing, whatever it looked like from outside."

midQuestBeats.label: Marion's knowledge is organized like a ledger, not a confession -- names, dates, and the specific financial instrument each one touched.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: This isn't gossip. It's six years of contract administration turned into something closer to a map of the Court's actual machinery.

midQuestBeats.label: She has been waiting to see whether you'd use this to build something or to settle a score.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Marion never said she doubted you. She also never said she didn't. The waiting was the test, not any question she actually asked.

title: What Brand Saw
briefing: Brand has been waiting months for a reason, or permission to stop waiting for one. Both are available now, if he's willing to talk about the night he disappeared into the Warrens instead of standing his ground.
openingText: "I didn't run like Sanna ran," Brand says, like the distinction matters more to him than it should. "I fought two of them at the gate. Lost, obviously. Woke up in the Warrens three days later with no memory of getting there and a debt to whoever dragged me." He rolls his shoulder, an old injury talking. "I can tell you what they looked like. I can tell you it wasn't the Court's usual muscle."
aftermathText: Brand's description matches the Gilded Hand's known roster closely enough to confirm what the tannery watch already suggested: professionals, not Court regulars, ran the operation from the first night. He tells it flatly, without asking for credit, and when he's done he straightens like something's been set down that he's been carrying since the gate. "That's the reason," he says. "Didn't need a new one. Just needed to say the old one out loud."

midQuestBeats.label: Brand's account of the gate fight is specific in ways a fabricated story usually isn't -- the number of attackers, the order they came at him.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Brand remembers the fight in the exact disordered way real memory works, not the way a rehearsed excuse would.

midQuestBeats.label: The debt he woke up owing traces to a Warrens fixer who has done work for the Gilded Hand before.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Brand's three lost days weren't an accident of the Warrens. Someone with Gilded Hand connections collected him, patched him up, and left a bill.

title: Not Running This Time
briefing: Sanna Veld asked for a chance to not run again. There's a dangerous errand that needs doing in Court-watched territory, and she's asked to be the one who does it.
openingText: Sanna is already packed by the time you agree. "I know the Pale better than anyone you'd send instead. I know where the Court patrols thin out and where they don't." She isn't asking for reassurance. She's asking to be trusted with something that matters, in a district she once fled through in the opposite direction.
aftermathText: She gets it done -- quietly, competently, exactly as promised, and comes back through the same streets she ran through the night the house fell. She doesn't say anything about that on the way in. She says something about it, briefly, on the way out, and then never brings it up again. Whatever she needed to prove, to you or to herself, reads as settled.

midQuestBeats.label: Sanna's route avoids every patrol pattern the house has logged so far -- she genuinely does know the Pale's blind spots.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: Sanna wasn't exaggerating. Her knowledge of the district's patrol gaps is better than anything in the house's own notes.

midQuestBeats.label: She passes within sight of the old house without slowing down, without looking away from the job either.
midQuestBeats.journalEntry: She walked past House Valdris and didn't run. That was never going to be in the mission report, but it happened, and it mattered.
